questions.statement: Akbar was born in 1542 in Amarkot Fort in present-day Sind.
questions.explanation: Akbar was indeed born in 1542 in Amarkot Fort in present-day Sind, while his father Humayun was in exile.

questions.statement: Akbar was 15 years old when he succeeded to the Mughal throne.
questions.explanation: Akbar was 13 years old, not 15, when he was forced to succeed to the throne after the sudden death of his father Humayun.

questions.statement: Bairam Khan served as the regent of Akbar from 1556 to 1560.
questions.explanation: Bairam Khan was Humayun's favourite and confidant who served as regent of Akbar from 1556 to 1560, with the title of Khan-i-Khanan.

questions.statement: The Second Battle of Panipat was fought on 5 November 1576.
questions.explanation: The Second Battle of Panipat was fought on 5 November 1556, not 1576. The Battle of Haldighati was fought in 1576.

questions.statement: Hemu was the general of Afghan emperor Adil Shah, who was Sher Shah's nephew.
questions.explanation: Hemu was indeed the general of Adil Shah, who was Sher Shah's nephew. Hemu seized Delhi and Agra before being defeated at the Second Battle of Panipat.

questions.statement: Akbar removed Bairam Khan when Akbar turned 21 years old.
questions.explanation: Akbar removed Bairam Khan when he turned 18, not 21. He asked Bairam Khan to go to Mecca.

questions.statement: Ahmadnagar was bravely defended by Chand Bibi and only part of it was annexed by the Mughals.
questions.explanation: When Akbar attacked Ahmadnagar in 1590, Chand Bibi bravely defended it, and the Mughals could only annex part of the territory.

questions.statement: Raja Birbal of Amber was the first Rajput to enter into an alliance with Akbar.
questions.explanation: It was Raja Bharmal of Amber, not Raja Birbal, who was the first Rajput to enter into a matrimonial alliance with Akbar.

questions.statement: The Battle of Haldighati was fought on 18 June 1576.
questions.explanation: The Battle of Haldighati was indeed fought on 18 June 1576 between the armies of Maharana Pratap and the Mughal forces led by Man Singh I of Amber.

questions.statement: Akbar personally led the Mughal forces at the Battle of Haldighati.
questions.explanation: Akbar did not personally lead the forces at Haldighati. The Mughal forces were led by Man Singh I of Amber.

questions.statement: Under the mansabdari system, ranks were expressed in numerical terms using Zat and Sawar.
questions.explanation: The mansab rank was denoted by two numbers: Zat (personal rank) and Sawar (size of cavalry contingent). They were not expressed in alphabetical terms.

questions.statement: The mansab rank was hereditary and could be passed on to the officer's children.
questions.explanation: The rank was NOT hereditary. It was held only during the mansabdar's lifetime, and after death, the jagir reverted to the emperor.

questions.statement: Raja Todar Mal was in charge of land revenue and assessment.
questions.explanation: Raja Todar Mal was in charge of land revenue and assessment and introduced the dahsaal or zabti system.

questions.statement: Fatehpur Sikri was a new capital city founded by Akbar.
questions.explanation: Akbar built Fatehpur Sikri as his new capital near Agra, selecting the site because the Sufi saint Salim Chisti lived there.

questions.statement: Raja Man Singh accepted Din-i-Ilahi openly.
questions.explanation: Raja Man Singh refused to accept Din-i-Ilahi. Only Raja Birbal accepted and followed it among Akbar's close associates.

questions.statement: Tansen was a famous musician and singer in Akbar's court.
questions.explanation: Tansen was one of the Navratnas and a famous musician who composed many Hindustani ragas including Raag Darbari and Deepak Raag.

questions.statement: Humayun's Tomb is located in Agra.
questions.explanation: Humayun's Tomb is located in New Delhi, not in Agra.

questions.statement: Akbar got the Ramayana and the Mahabharata translated from Sanskrit to Persian.
questions.explanation: Akbar promoted cultural exchange by getting the Ramayana and the Mahabharata translated from Sanskrit to Persian.

questions.statement: Akbar learnt to read and write fluently and was a great scholar.
questions.explanation: Akbar never learnt to read or write, but he was fond of knowledge and had books read to him.

questions.statement: Akbar prohibited the practice of Sati and legalised widow remarriage.
questions.explanation: As part of his social reforms, Akbar prohibited the practice of Sati and legalised widow remarriage.
